title: The Phillips curve: linking changes in the AD-AS model to the Phillips curve
description: In this exercise, practice using the graphical model of the Phillips curve
topic_path: The Phillips curve: linking changes in the AD-AS model to the Phillips curve

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following represents how a negative demand shock would impact the Phillips curve model?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A graph featuring a downward-sloping curve labeled "SRPC" represents the short-run Phillips curve, intersected by a vertical line at point "1," which indicates a specific value on the horizontal axis. A second vertical line at point "2" illustrates a different value, aligned with the dashed horizontal lines indicating two different levels on the vertical axis, labeled as "UR%" for unemployment rate percentage. The axes are marked with dashed lines. The horizontal axis extends from left to right, while the vertical axis extends from bottom to top.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/abf51beaa8701d7ffaaf33566abdb21db75e7c0f)

![A rectangular grid with a vertical axis representing numerical values ranging from zero to a specified maximum, and a horizontal axis indicating a separate variable, displays a continuous curve that initially rises steeply and eventually levels off, with key points marked along the curve indicating precise numerical values corresponding to both axes.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e45a6323b70a8165f36cdfa24801c31b7480eedc)

![The image presents a graph with two downward-sloping lines labeled "Long-Run Phillips Curve" and two short-run Phillips Curves labeled "Short-Run Phillips Curve 1" and "Short-Run Phillips Curve 2." The vertical axis is labeled "inflation percentage," and the horizontal axis is labeled "unemployment percentage." There are two horizontal dashed lines intersecting the "Long-Run Phillips Curve" at points marked “1” and “2.” A blue dashed line extends downward from point “2” to the horizontal axis, indicating the relationship between inflation and unemployment in this economic model.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ea41a732933ffe1d6f79360a24b2affffb38fe03)

![The visual consists of a Cartesian coordinate system featuring two axes. The vertical axis represents inflation percentage labeled as "inf. %" and the horizontal axis represents unemployment percentage labeled as "UR %." There are three distinct lines: one labeled as "LRPC," which is vertical, and two labeled as "SRPC1" and "SRPC2," both of which slope downward to the right. The horizontal line corresponding to "LRPC" intersects the vertical line at a specific point marked as "1." Additionally, there are two dashed horizontal lines extending from the intersection of "SRPC1" and "SRPC2," with a vertical dashed line pointing to a second point marked "2." The graph does not include color distinctions in its labeling.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e80ad18daaab40781980207bd7c0dddcaf1b27c8)

![The image contains a graph with the vertical axis labeled "inflation percentage" and the horizontal axis labeled "unemployment rate percentage." A downward-sloping line represents the Short-Run Phillips Curve, denoted as "SRPC," indicating an inverse relationship between inflation and unemployment. A second line labeled "Long-Run Phillips Curve," denoted as "LRPC," is horizontal at a constant inflation percentage. Two points are marked on the SRPC: point 1 is located above point 2, with both points connected by a vertical dashed line. The vertical dashed line indicates the shift from point 1 to point 2 along the unemployment rate percentage axis. There are horizontal dashed lines extending from point 1 and point 2 to emphasize their corresponding inflation values.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0fdf246aa0701d838d85fd14e6faa127487f6082)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following represents how a positive aggregate demand shock would impact the Phillips curve model?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![The image is a vertical line graph with a single curve that decreases steadily from the upper left to the lower right. The x-axis represents time, divided into equal intervals, while the y-axis indicates the measured value, which ranges from a higher starting point down to zero. The curve appears smooth, with no abrupt changes or jagged edges, indicating a consistent decrease over the observed time. The graph includes labeled axes and a title positioned above the graph, providing context for the data presented.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e45a6323b70a8165f36cdfa24801c31b7480eedc)

![A line graph displaying a downward-sloping curve labeled "SRPC," plotted against a horizontal axis labeled "UR%" (Unemployment Rate Percentage) and a vertical axis indicating a numerical scale. The curve intersects the vertical axis at point one, with a dashed line extending horizontally to the right, indicating a specific unemployment rate, while a dashed line from point two drops vertically to meet the horizontal axis. A red arrow indicates a downward movement from point two to point one along the dashed line.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/abf51beaa8701d7ffaaf33566abdb21db75e7c0f)

![A two-dimensional graph with the vertical axis labeled "inflation percentage" and the horizontal axis labeled "unemployment percentage." The graph contains two downward-sloping lines representing "short-run Phillips curve one" (SRPC one) and "short-run Phillips curve two" (SRPC two). A horizontal line labeled "long-run Phillips curve" (LRPC) intersects both short-run curves at a marked point labeled "2" below the LRPC. A vertical dashed line from the point of intersection extends down to the horizontal axis, intersecting the unemployment percentage axis at a labeled point "1." An arrow indicates the direction from the LRPC to SRPC two.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ea41a732933ffe1d6f79360a24b2affffb38fe03)

![A graph featuring two downward-sloping lines labeled as Short-Run Phillips Curve one and Short-Run Phillips Curve two, with a horizontal line labeled as Long-Run Phillips Curve. The vertical axis is labeled as inflation percentage, while the horizontal axis is labeled as unemployment rate percentage. Two marked points on the graph, labeled as one and two, are indicated by solid circles. A dashed vertical line intersects point two, demonstrating the relationship between the two axes.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/82783effa547eacd530f6ee4f85f2f5aad68627d)

![A downward-sloping line representing the Short-Run Phillips Curve labeled "SRPC" extends from the upper left to the lower right of the graph, intersecting two horizontal dashed lines labeled "LRPC" at the left and at point one indicated with a solid black dot. A vertical dashed line intersects the SRPC at point two marked by a solid black dot, extending to the x-axis labeled "UR%" and the y-axis labeled "inf. %". A red downward arrow highlights the shift from point one to point two.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0fdf246aa0701d838d85fd14e6faa127487f6082)

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy shown here was initially in long-run equilibrium, but aggregate demand increased from AD1 to AD2.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/073558aca2f60398b250b82e2319fa5509bbea2b)

**Which of the following graphs reflects what happens in the Phillips curve model as a result of the change shown here?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A chart with a horizontal axis labeled "Time" and a vertical axis labeled "Value" features two distinct lines representing data sets over time; the first line rises steadily, peaking at the halfway point, then gradually declines, while the second line fluctuates with sharp peaks and troughs, remaining below the first line throughout its course; both lines display visible markers at each data point.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e45a6323b70a8165f36cdfa24801c31b7480eedc)

![A graph with a downward-sloping line labeled "SRPC" on the right side, depicting a relationship between two variables. The horizontal axis is labeled "UR%" and shows values ranging from left to right. A vertical dashed line intersects the horizontal axis at a point marked "1" and extends upward to meet the downward slope. Additionally, a second vertical dashed line intersects the horizontal axis at a point marked "2," with an arrow pointing down from this point to the horizontal axis. There are dashed horizontal lines extending from the intersection points to the left, indicating values on the vertical axis. The graph has no numerical labels or additional context provided.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/abf51beaa8701d7ffaaf33566abdb21db75e7c0f)

![A graph with a horizontal axis labeled "UR%" at the bottom representing an unspecified percentage and a vertical axis labeled "inf. %" on the left representing inflation percentage. A downward-sloping line labeled "LRPC" moves from the upper left to the lower right. Two horizontal dashed lines intersect the vertical axis at points labeled "1" and "2," respectively. A vertical dashed line extends from point "2" down to the horizontal axis, intersecting the "SRPC" line. There are annotations indicating the relationship between the lines at the intersection points.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4b34e972935908eae08f60c0d6caae9e98f908ee)

![A two-dimensional graph with the vertical axis labeled "inflation percentage" and the horizontal axis labeled "unemployment percentage." The graph includes three lines: a horizontal line labeled "Long Run Phillips Curve" (L R P C) and two downward-sloping lines labeled "Short Run Phillips Curve one" (S R P C one) and "Short Run Phillips Curve two" (S R P C two). The downward slope of the "Short Run Phillips Curve one" is steeper than that of the "Short Run Phillips Curve two." There are two points marked as "1" and "2," with a dashed vertical line extending from point "2" to meet the horizontal axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/82783effa547eacd530f6ee4f85f2f5aad68627d)

![A graph with labeled axes showing the relationship between unemployment rate percentage on the horizontal axis and inflation percentage on the vertical axis. A downward-sloping line labeled "Long Run Phillips Curve" is shown, intersecting two downward-sloping lines labeled "Short Run Phillips Curve one" and "Short Run Phillips Curve two." There are two dashed horizontal lines at points labeled "2" and "1," indicating specific values on the inflation axis. A vertical dashed line connects point "1" on the horizontal axis to the inflation axis, indicating the relationship between unemployment and inflation rates.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ea41a732933ffe1d6f79360a24b2affffb38fe03)

assessment_items.question_content:
In the graph of the Phillips curve below, an economy moved from point 1 to point 2 to point 3.

**Which of the following would be the most likely reason for the movement from point 2 to point 3 shown in this graph?**

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/614fcb4009ad419c39a775d249173db9182c904c)

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A decrease in short-run aggregate supply (SRAS)
A decrease in aggregate demand (AD)
An increase in aggregate demand
A change in the natural rate of unemployment
An increase in short-run aggregate supply (SRAS)

assessment_items.question_content:
In the graph of the Phillips curve shown here, an economy moved from point 1 to point 2 to point 3.

**Which of the following would best represent the movement from point 2 to point 3 shown in the graph?**

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/35f03576d54e754df746dd005063ffde5167336f)

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in short-run aggregate supply (SRAS)
A decrease in short-run aggregate supply (SRAS)
An increase in aggregate demand (AD)
A decrease in aggregate demand (AD)
An increase in the natural rate of unemployment

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following would cause the long-run Phillips curve (LRPC) to shift to the left?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A decrease in the natural rate of unemployment
An increase in the natural rate of unemployment
An increase in aggregate demand (AD)
A decrease in aggregate demand (AD)
An increase in short-run aggregate supply (SRAS)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following would cause the long-run Phillips curve to shift to the right?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in structural unemployment
A decrease in frictional unemployment
An increase in cyclical unemployment
An increase in aggregate demand (AD)
An decrease in aggregate demand (AD)

assessment_items.question_content:
The current rate of unemployment in Dodgeville is 8% and the natural rate of unemployment is 3%. Inflation is currently 2%. 

**Which of the following correctly shows a long-run Phillip curve (LRPC) based on this information?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A two-dimensional graph with a vertical and horizontal axis. The vertical axis is labeled "inflation percentage" and extends upwards. The horizontal axis is labeled "unemployment rate percentage" and extends to the right. A vertical line is drawn at the three percent mark on the horizontal axis, and a line labeled "LRPC" extends upwards from this point without intersecting the horizontal axis. The overall graph is framed within a rectangle.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ac8ffe9cec9d90cac1468a222754154ed5875cd4)

![A two-dimensional graph with the vertical axis labeled "inflation percentage" ranging from zero to a specified value, and the horizontal axis labeled "unemployment percentage" with values extending from zero. A horizontal line represents "LRPC" positioned at the two percent mark on the vertical axis, intersecting the horizontal axis at an unspecified value labeled "UR percentage." The graph is enclosed in a rectangle with a defined dimension of 292 by 311 units.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cf49c77dd28df63651d0645d537e49c2b59f7ad1)

![A two-dimensional graph with a vertical axis labeled "inflation percentage" ranging from zero to an unspecified upper limit and a horizontal axis labeled "unemployment percentage" with a mark at 8 percent. A vertical line labeled "Long Run Phillips Curve" extends upward from the 8 percent unemployment mark, intersecting the inflation percentage axis. The graph has a bounding box around it, and dimensions noted as 292 by 311.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/94402f74c2d8f8642024f07c5a0b6e238e5a8a28)

![A line graph with a vertical axis labeled "inf lation percentage" ranging from 2% to 8% and a horizontal axis labeled "U R percentage" ranging from 3% to 8% shows a dashed horizontal line extending from the point (3%, 2%) to the vertical line at inf lation, intersecting at the value of 2%. It then rises steeply to reach a solid line segment labeled "L R P C" at the intersection of 8% on the U R percentage axis and approximately 6% on the inf lation percentage axis. The graph indicates a stepwise increase in the relationship between the two variables, with a visible break in slope between the points.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f9d11f06bb93f62e4279b5e80b7abfec1065dc78)

![A graph with an x-axis labeled "UR%" ranging from 0% to 8%, and a y-axis labeled "inf. %" ranging from 0% to 2%. The graph features a downward-sloping line that intersects the y-axis slightly above 2% and extends to the right where it crosses the dotted horizontal line at the x-axis at the point labeled "LRPC." There is a dashed horizontal line at the level of 2% on the y-axis. An additional label for "UR%" extends horizontally at the x-coordinate of 8%.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ce3130bda96bc3aab67ab3cbf701dd096779d9b4)

assessment_items.question_content:
**What causes the long-run Phillips curve to shift?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Changes in structural or frictional unemployment
Changes in cyclical unemployment
Changes in expectations about inflation
Shifts in the aggregate demand curve
Shifts in the short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve

assessment_items.question_content:
In the graph of the Phillips curve shown here, an economy moved from point 1 to point 2 to point 3.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/614fcb4009ad419c39a775d249173db9182c904c)


**Which of the following would cause the movement from point 2 to point 3 shown in this graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Higher expected inflation
Cheaper labor
Cheaper natural resources
Expansionary monetary policy
A lower natural rate of unemployment

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following actions would most likely lead to a shift to the left of the short-run Phillips curve, or SRPC?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A strong central bank commitment to reducing inflation
A strong central bank commitment to decreasing cyclical unemployment
A strong government commitment to engage in expansionary fiscal policy
A strong central bank commitment to increasing the money supply
A strong government commitment to reducing structural unemployment

assessment_items.question_content:
The natural rate of unemployment in Elizaland is 5%. The citizens of Elizaland expected inflation to be 2% and unemployment to be 5% during 2018. However, inflation turned out to be 6% and unemployment 1.5%.

**What happens in the long run as people begin to expect a permanently higher rate of inflation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The short-run Phillips curve (SRPC) shifts to the right
The short-run Phillips curve (SPRC) shifts to the left.
The long-run Phillips curve (LRPC) shifts to the right
The long-run Phillips curve (LRPC) shifts to the left
A movement to the right along the short-run Phillips curve (SRPC)
